Abstract – Finding accurate information from long PDF 
documents is often time - consuming, and many AI 
syst ems provide answers without reliable proof. To solve 
this, we developed an AI PDF Question Answering 
System that allows users to upload one or more PDFs 
and ask questions through text or voice. The system 
extracts document text page by page, splits it into 
meaningful chunks, and stores metadata such as file 
name, page number, paragraph, line range, and chunk 
ID. For answering, it combines semantic retrieval 
(embedding similarity) with lexical matching to improve 
precision for IDs, numbers, and record - style queries. 
The final answer is generated by a constrained language 
model using only retrieved context, and each response 
includes exact source citations for transparency. The 
system also supports downloadable PDF reports 
containing the question, answer, and cited sources. By 
grounding outputs in uploaded documents, the system 
reduces hallucinations, improves trust, and speeds up 
document analysis workflows. 
 
Index Terms – Artificial Intelligence, Retrieval - 
Augmented Generation (RAG), PDF Question 
Answering, Semantic Search, Embeddings, Source 
Citation, Document Intelligence, Explainable AI. 
I NTRODUCTION 
In today’s digital world, a large amount of information is 
stored in PDF documents such as reports, research papers, 
invoices, manuals, and official records. Finding exact 
answers from these files is often difficult and time - 
consuming, especially when user s need precise 
references. Most traditional search methods only match 
keywords and do not understand the meaning of the 
question, while many AI systems may generate responses 
without proper proof. To address this problem, this 
project presents an AI PDF Qu estion Answering System 
that provides accurate, context - based answers with clear 
source citations. 
 
The system allows users to upload one or more PDF files 
and ask questions using text or voice. After upload, the 
backend extracts text page by page, divides it into 
meaningful chunks, and stores metadata such as file 
name, page number, paragraph number, line range, and 
chunk ID. For retrieval, the system uses embedding - based 
semantic search (cosine similarity) and also applies 
lexical/exact token matching for values like IDs, numbers, 
and record - specific queries. This hybrid approach 
improves both relevance and precision of retrieved 
content. 
 
The retrieved chunks are then passed to an answer 
generation model (Gemini or local LLM), where the 
model is constrain ed to respond only from the provided 
context. The output includes both the final answer and 
cited chunk IDs, which are mapped back to exact source 
locations for transparency. The frontend displays the 
answer with citation cards, and users can download a 
cl ean PDF report containing the question, answer, and top 
cited sources. Overall, this system reduces hallucination, 
increases trust in AI - generated responses, and makes 
document analysis faster and more reliable. 
 
L ITERATURE R EVIEW 
Recent research shows th at modern question answering 
systems perform best when they combine strong retrieval,

language understanding, and grounded generation. Endait 
et al. [1] introduced IndicSQuAD, a multilingual QA 
dataset for Indic languages, highlighting the need for 
robust QA models in low - resource and diverse language 
settings. Vats et al. [2] further explored structured QA in 
Indic languages using multilingual state - space models, 
indicating that architecture design can improve 
performance for multilingual and format - sensit ive 
queries. Terdalkar and Bhattacharya [3] proposed a 
Sanskrit QA framework using knowledge graph 
construction, demonstrating that domain structure and 
symbolic representations can support more accurate 
reasoning in specialized languages. 
 
For semantic re trieval, Reimers and Gurevych [4] 
proposed Sentence - BERT, which enables efficient 
sentence - level embeddings and similarity search. Khattab 
and Zaharia [5] introduced ColBERT, improving passage 
ranking through late interaction, which is useful for 
precise r etrieval in QA pipelines. Bandyopadhyay et al. 
[6] presented IITP’s QA - related system report in 
MEDIQA, showing practical methods for question 
entailment and answer selection in applied settings. Amin 
et al. [7] addressed Marathi QA with deep learning, 
rei nforcing the importance of language - specific 
adaptation for Indian contexts. 
 
In document - focused QA, Mathew et al. [8] introduced 
DocVQA, a benchmark for understanding document 
images, while Appalaraju et al. [9] proposed DocFormer, 
an end - to - end transfor mer for document understanding 
that combines textual and layout cues. These works are 
directly relevant to PDF - based QA systems where 
structure and content both matter. Finally, Asai et al. [10] 
proposed Self - RAG, where models retrieve, generate, and 
self - critique, improving faithfulness and reducing 
unsupported responses. 
 
Based on these studies, the key design direction for this 
project is clear: use embedding - based retrieval with 
strong ranking, handle document structure effectively, and 
generate answers only from retrieved evidence with 
transparent citations. This directly motivates the 
architecture of the proposed AI PDF Question Answering 
system. 
P ROPOSED M ETHODOLOGY 
The proposed system workflow of our AI PDF Question 
Answering Platform consists of mu ltiple stages, from user 
query intake to citation - grounded answer generation and 
PDF report export . 
 
Fig. 1 Proposed System Architecture 
 
I. Data Collection and Document Ingestion 
Data collection begins when the user uploads one or more 
PDFs (research papers , reports, academic documents, 
etc.). 
Each uploaded document is stored in a multi - PDF 
repository. The backend then performs page - wise text 
extraction and converts raw PDF content into machine - 
readable textual units. 
 
For each extracted unit, metadata is preserved: 
 
 File name 
 Page number 
 Paragraph ID 
 Line range 
 Chunk ID 
 
This metadata - centric ingestion is essential for traceable 
citation generation in later stages. 
 
II. Data Preprocessing 
Raw extracted PDF text may contain noise (extra spaces, 
broken lines, headers/footers, encoding artifacts). 
A preprocessing pipeline is applied to improve retrieval 
quality: 
 
 Text cleaning and normalization 
 Tokenization 
 Chunk segmentation (small paragraph units) 
 Optional semantic chunk merging for context 
continuity 
 Metadata alignment for source tracking 
For voice queries, Speech - to - Text conversion is 
performed before preprocessing.

III. Feature Representation (Embeddings) 
Each chunk is converted into a dense vector 
representation using an embedding model: 
 
Local embedding service (127.0.0.1:8000) or 
Gemini embedding API 
 
Similarly, the user query is embedded into the same 
vector space, enabling semantic similarity search. 
 
IV. Query Processing Layer 
The user submits a question in text or voice form. 
The query processing layer includes: 
 
A. Speech - to - Text (if voice input) 
B. Query cleaning and tokenization 
C. Intent/context understanding 
D. Embedding generation for query 
representation 
 
This stage standardizes user input before retrieval. 
 
V. Information Retrieval (Hybrid Search) 
T he system performs hybrid retrieval using semantic 
similarity and lexical matching. 
For query vector q and chunk vector ci,cosine similarity 
is : 
 
A hybrid score is computed as: 
Si=α ⋅ sim(q,ci)+(1−α) ⋅ lexical 
Where: 
 α = sema ntic weight 
 lexical(q,ci)= exact token/keyword 
overlap score 
Top - k chunks with highest scores ar e selected as evidence 
context. This improves performance for both semantic 
questions and record - style queries (IDs, numbers, 
entities). 
VI. Answer Generation and Citation Mapping 
The retrieved chu nks are passed to the answer model 
(Gemini/local LLM) with a strict grounding prompt: 
generate answers only from retrieved context. 
 
Model output format includes: 
 
 Final answer text 
 Cited chunkIds (JSON) 
Backend then maps cited chunk IDs to source metadata 
(file/page/paragraph/line), and frontend displays: 
 
 Answer block 
 Source cards with exact provenance 
 This ensures transparency and reduces 
hallucinations. 
. 
Outputs

R EFERENCES 
1. Sharvi Endait, Ruturaj Ghatage, Aditya Kulkarni, 
Rajlaxmi Patil, and Raviraj Joshi. “IndicSQuAD: A 
Comprehensive Multilingual Question Answering 
Dataset for Indic Languages.” 
 
2. Arpita Vats, Rahul Raja, Mrinal Mathur, Aman 
Chadha, and Vinija Jain. “Multilingual State Space 
Models for Structured Question An swering in Indic 
Languages.” In LoResMT 2025 (ACL Workshop) . 
 
3. Hrishikesh Terdalkar and Arnab Bhattacharya. 
“Framework for Question - Answering in Sanskrit 
through Automated Construction of Knowledge 
Graphs.” In ISCLS 2019 (ACL Anthology). 
 
4. Nils Reimers and I ryna Gurevych. “Sentence - BERT: 
Sentence Embeddings using Siamese BERT - 
Networks.” In Proceedings of EMNLP - IJCNLP 2019. 
 
5. Omar Khattab and Matei Zaharia. “ColBERT: 
Efficient and Effective Passage Search via 
Contextualized Late Interaction over BERT.” In 
Proceedings of SIGIR 2020. 
 
6. Dibyanayan Bandyopadhyay, Baban Gain, Tanik 
Saikh, and Asif Ekbal. “IITP at MEDIQA 2019: 
Systems Report for Natural Language Inference, 
Question Entailment and Question Answering.” In 
BioNLP Workshop 2019. 
 
7. Dhiraj Amin, Sharvari Govilkar, and Sagar Kulkarni. 
“Question answering using deep learning in low 
resource Indian language Marathi.” 
 
8. Minesh Mathew, Dimosthenis Karatzas, and C. V. 
Jawahar. “DocVQA: A Dataset for VQA on 
Document Images.” In Proceedings of WACV 2021. 
 
 
9. Srikar Appalaraju, Bhavan Jasani, Bhargava Urala 
Kota, Yusheng Xie, and R. Manmatha. “DocFormer: 
End - to - End Transformer for Document 
Understanding.” In Proceedings of ICCV 2021. 
 
10. Akari Asai, Zeqiu Wu, Yizhong Wang, Avi Sil, and 
Hannaneh Hajishirzi. “Self - RAG: Lea rning to 
Retrieve, Generate, and Critique through Self - 
Reflection.” In International Conference on Learning 
Representations (ICLR 2024).

Project Synopsis 
1. Introduction 
This project is a smart AI - based knowledge retrieval platform designed to help users find accurate 
information from multiple PDF documents using voice and text queries. The system processes large 
collections of documents such as research paper s, reports, and academic files, and extracts relevant 
information based on user queries. By understanding the context and meaning of questions, the platform 
provides precise and reliable answers, reducing the time and effort required for manual searching. 
The system is powered by Artificial Intelligence and Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques. It 
integrates speech recognition, semantic document analysis, and intelligent answer generation to deliver 
context - aware responses with proper references. By supporting multilingual input and output, the platform 
aims to improve accessibility, enhance research efficiency, and support users across academic, corporate, 
and professional domains. 
 
2. Literature Review 
 
Paper Title& Year Focus of the Paper Key Findings Relevance to Our Topic 
 
 
 
Beyond Cascading 
Speech Recognition 
with Text Retrieval 
(2015) 
 
Integrated Automatic 
Speech Recognition 
(ASR) with text - based

Supports semantic processing

improving accurate information

such as POS tagging for 
question generation and

documents.

improves semantic

– Effective processing

Provides NLP techniques useful

answer generation in our AI - 
based knowledge retrieval 
system.

Vishwakarma Institute of Technology Issue01 :Rev No.00:Dt. 01/08/22 
 
 
 
 
A Survey on Question 
Answering Systems Based 
on Deep Learning (2018) 
 
 
Reviewed deep 
learning - based 
question answering 
systems. 
– Deep learning 
outperforms rule - based 
QA systems. 
– Context - aware models 
improve answer 
relevance. 
– Highlights evolution of 
NLP - based QA methods. 
 
 
Provides foundat ional 
knowledge for AI - driven 
question answering used in 
our project. 
 
3. Problem Statement 
Users often face difficulties in finding accurate and relevant information from large collections of PDF 
documents due to the limitations of traditional keyword - based search methods. Manually searching through 
multiple documents is time - consuming and inefficient, especially when precise answers with proper 
references are required. Existing systems lack support for voice - based interaction and multilingual querying, 
redu cing accessibility for many users. There is a need for an intelligent digital platform that enables voice 
and text - based querying, understands contextual meaning, and retrieves reliable, referenced information 
from multiple documents. This project aims to solve this problem by providing an AI - driven, user - friendly 
knowledge retrieval system that improves efficiency, accuracy, and accessibility. 
4. Objectives 
1. To develop an AI - based digital platform for retrieving accurate information from multiple PDF 
documents. 
2. To enable voice and text - based querying for improved user accessibility. 
3. To provide context - aware and semantic search using Natural Language Processing techniques. 
4. To generate precise answers along with document references such as page and parag raph numbers. 
5. To simplify knowledge access through a user - friendly and efficient interface. . 
 
5. Work flow of Texta Platform 
 
1. User Input Layer – Accepts user queries in text or voice format through a simple and interactive 
interface. 
2. Speech - to - Text Module – C onverts voice queries into text using Automatic Speech Recognition 
(ASR). 
3. Query Processing Unit – Cleans, tokenizes, and interprets the user query using NLP techniques. 
4. Semantic Understanding Engine – Analyzes user intent and context using transformer - based 
language models. 
5. Document Repository (Knowledge Base) – Stores multiple PDF documents such as research papers, 
reports, and academic files. 
6. Information Retrieval Module – Searches and identifies r elevant sections across multiple PDFs using 
semantic search. 
7. Answer Extraction Engine – Extracts precise answers along with page and paragraph references for 
credibility. 
8. Multilingual Processing Unit – Translates queries and responses to support multiple l anguages. 
9. Output Generation Module – Converts retrieved information into structured, easy - to - understand 
responses. . 
10. Frontend Interface – Web - based dashboard that allows users to submit queries and view answers 
with references easily.

Vishwakarma Institute of Technology Issue01 :Rev No.00:Dt. 01/08/22

Vishwakarma Institute of Technology Issue01 :Rev No.00:Dt. 01/08/22 
 
 
6. Tools and Technologies Used 
 
The development of Texta: AI - Based Knowledge Retrieval Using Voice and Text Queries involves a 
combination of modern web technologies, Natural Language Processing techniques, and AI tools to ensure 
accurate information retrieval, scalability, and ease of use. 
 
1. HTML5 , CSS3,JavaScript,and TailwindCSS – 
Used to design the frontend interface, making it responsive, visually appealing, and easy to use for 
students, researchers, and professionals. 
 
2. EJS(Embedded JavaScript Templates) – 
Enables dynamic rendering of webpages and displays query results and document references in real 
time . 
 
3. Speech Rec ognition API – 
Used to convert voice queries into text using Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR), allowing 
hands - free interaction with the system. 
 
4. Node.js and Express.js – 
Handle backend logic, manage API routes, and connect frontend inputs with document processing and 
AI modules. . 
 
5. Document Parsing Libraries (PDF.js / PyPDF / similar) – 
Used to extract text, page numbers, and paragraph information from multiple PDF documents. 
 
6. Natural Language Processing (NLP) Models – 
Transformer - based models are used f or semantic understanding, intent detection, and contextual 
analysis of user queries. 
 
7. Semantic Search Techniques – 
Used to match user queries with relevant document sections across multiple PDFs based on 
meaning rather than keywords. 
 
8. Multilingual Translation API – 
Supports multilingual input and output, enabling users to query and receive answers in different 
languages. 
 
9. MongoDB Atlas Cloud – 
Stores document metadata, extracted text, embeddings, and query history securely, ensuring fast 
access and scalability. 
 
10. Git and GitHub – 
Used for version control, collaboration, and maintaining project updates among all team members.
